message:
feat(sqlmodel): field handling and validation improvements

- field.rs: Enhanced field metadata and type mapping
- validate.rs: Runtime validation utilities
- validate_derive.rs: Compile-time validation in derive macros
- Beads issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -136,7 +136,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-h0n","title":"Implement schema metadata (title, description, schema_extra)","description":"## Description

Add support for schema metadata fields for JSON Schema generation.

## Python Behavior

```python
class User(SQLModel):
    name: str = Field(
        title='User Name',
        description='The full name of the user',
        schema_extra={'examples': ['John Doe']}
    )
```

## Rust Implementation

### Macro Attributes
```rust
#[derive(Model)]
struct User {
    #[sqlmodel(
        title = "User Name",
        description = "The full name of the user",
        schema_extra = r#"{"examples": ["John Doe"]}"#
    )]
    name: String,
}
```

### JSON Schema Generation

Implement JsonSchema trait or method:
```rust
impl User {
    fn json_schema() -> serde_json::Value {
        // Generate JSON Schema with title, description, examples
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] title attribute parsed and stored
- [ ] description attribute parsed and stored
- [ ] schema_extra parsed as JSON and merged
- [ ] json_schema() method generates correct schema
- [ ] Works with schemars if we add that dep, or custom impl

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/field.rs)
- [ ] Test title parsed
- [ ] Test description parsed
- [ ] Test schema_extra merged
- [ ] Test examples parsed

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/schema_metadata.rs)
- [ ] JSON Schema includes title
- [ ] JSON Schema includes description
- [ ] schema_extra adds custom keys
- [ ] examples in schema
- [ ] OpenAPI schema generation

### Logging
- [ ] TRACE: Schema metadata extraction

-{"id":"bd-iy1","title":"Implement collection validators (min_items, max_items, unique_items)","description":"## Description

Add validation for collection (Vec, array) fields.

## Python Behavior

```python
class Order(SQLModel):
    items: List[str] = Field(
        min_items=1,        # At least 1 item
        max_items=100,      # At most 100 items
        unique_items=True   # No duplicates
    )
```

## Rust Implementation

### Macro Attributes
```rust
#[derive(Model, Validate)]
struct Order {
    #[validate(min_items = 1, max_items = 100, unique_items)]
    items: Vec<String>,
}
```

### Generated Validation
```rust
if self.items.len() < 1 {
    errors.add_min_items("items", 1, self.items.len());
}
if self.items.len() > 100 {
    errors.add_max_items("items", 100, self.items.len());
}
if self.items.len() != self.items.iter().collect::<HashSet<_>>().len() {
    errors.add_unique_items("items");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] min_items enforces minimum collection size
- [ ] max_items enforces maximum collection size
- [ ] unique_items ensures no duplicates
- [ ] Works with Vec, HashSet, arrays
- [ ] Clear error messages

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/validate_derive.rs)
- [ ] Test min_items validation
- [ ] Test max_items validation
- [ ] Test unique_items validation
- [ ] Test combined constraints

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/collection_validators.rs)
- [ ] Vec with min_items=1 rejects empty
- [ ] Vec with max_items=10 rejects 11
- [ ] unique_items rejects duplicates
- [ ] All constraints on same field
- [ ] Nested collection validation

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Collection validation
- [ ] TRACE: Item count checking

 {"id":"bd-jrd","title":"Implement UNION, UNION ALL set operations","description":"## Description

Support UNION and UNION ALL to combine query results.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlalchemy import union, union_all

# UNION (removes duplicates)
stmt = union(
    select(User).where(User.type == 'admin'),
    select(User).where(User.type == 'superuser')
)

# UNION ALL (keeps duplicates)
stmt = union_all(query1, query2, query3)

result = session.exec(stmt)
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
// Method chaining
let query = select!(User)
    .filter(User::type_.eq("admin"))
    .union(
        select!(User).filter(User::type_.eq("superuser"))
    );

// Function style
let query = union([query1, query2, query3]);
let query = union_all([query1, query2]);
```

### SQL Generation

```sql
(SELECT ... FROM users WHERE type = 'admin')
UNION
(SELECT ... FROM users WHERE type = 'superuser')
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] union() method on Select
- [ ] union_all() method on Select
- [ ] Standalone union(), union_all() functions
- [ ] Works with different SELECT columns (must match)
- [ ] Correct SQL generation for all dialects
- [ ] Can chain multiple unions

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-query/src/set_ops.rs)
- [ ] Test UNION generation
- [ ] Test UNION ALL generation
- [ ] Test column compatibility check
- [ ] Test with ORDER BY/LIMIT

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/query_union.rs)
- [ ] UNION removes duplicates
- [ ] UNION ALL keeps duplicates
- [ ] UNION with different queries
- [ ] UNION with ORDER BY at end
- [ ] UNION subquery in FROM

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: UNION clause SQL generation

 {"id":"bd-kgpu","title":"Implement bulk operations (bulk_insert, bulk_update)","description":"## Description

Efficient bulk insert/update without individual object tracking.

## Python Behavior

```python
# Bulk insert without object tracking
session.bulk_insert_mappings(User, [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
])

# Bulk update
session.bulk_update_mappings(User, [
    {'id': 1, 'name': 'Alice Updated'},
    {'id': 2, 'name': 'Bob Updated'},
])

# Bulk save objects
session.bulk_save_objects([user1, user2, user3])
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
impl Session {
    pub async fn bulk_insert<M: Model>(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        models: &[M],
    ) -> Outcome<u64, Error> {
        // Single INSERT with multiple rows
        // INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES 
        //   ('Alice', 'alice@...'),
        //   ('Bob', 'bob@...')
    }
    
    pub async fn bulk_update<M: Model>(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        models: &[M],
    ) -> Outcome<u64, Error> {
        // Use CASE WHEN for batch update or multiple statements
    }
}
```

### Optimizations

- Batch into chunks (avoid huge queries)
- Use COPY for PostgreSQL bulk load
- Use LOAD DATA for MySQL

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] bulk_insert with efficient SQL
- [ ] bulk_update with efficient SQL
- [ ] Batch size configurable
- [ ] Returns affected row count
- [ ] Skips object tracking overhead

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-core/src/bulk.rs)
- [ ] Test bulk_insert generates multi-value INSERT
- [ ] Test bulk_update generates CASE/WHEN or multi-UPDATE
- [ ] Test batch size configuration
- [ ] Test empty collection handling
- [ ] Test large batch chunking

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/bulk_operations.rs)
- [ ] bulk_insert 1000 records → single statement
- [ ] bulk_update 1000 records efficiently
- [ ] bulk_insert with returning IDs
- [ ] bulk_insert with FK validation
- [ ] Performance vs individual inserts (10x+ faster)

### Logging
- [ ] INFO: Bulk operation size
- [ ] DEBUG: Batch chunking details
- [ ] TRACE: Individual record processing
